children.text: The C68 is Colnago's flagship road bike, hand-built in Italy and engineered for riders who want a machine that responds to every input. Carbon lugs joined to monocoque sections give the frame a classic Colnago silhouette with modern stiffness and compliance.
Equipped with Shimano Dura-Ace Di2, deep-section carbon wheels, and an integrated cockpit. Reserved for our most accomplished guests on the Colnago Tuscany itinerary.

The Roadmachine is BMC's endurance road platform, balancing all-day comfort with the precision Swiss engineering you would expect. Generous tire clearance handles strade bianche and patched chipseal without complaint.
Configured with Shimano Ultegra Di2 and 32 mm tires, the Roadmachine is our default choice for guests who want a refined, confidence-inspiring ride across mixed Tuscan terrain.

The Dogma F is the bike of Grand Tour winners, built in Treviso and refined across a generation of professional racing. Asymmetric tubing and Torayca carbon deliver a frame that punches up steep gradients and tracks cleanly through fast descents.
Built with Shimano Dura-Ace Di2 and Princeton CarbonWorks wheels. Available to guests with road racing experience who want to ride what the pros ride.

departures.notes: Spring departure. Vines breaking bud, ideal temperatures for climbing.
Early summer. Long days, fireflies in the vineyards at dusk.
Harvest season. Cellar visits coincide with the vendemmia.

title: Colnago Tuscany Bike Tour
subtitle: Six days through Chianti and the Crete Senesi on hand-built Italian carbon

children.text: Tuscany is the place cycling was made for. Cypress-lined ridges, family vineyards a thousand years in the making, hilltop villages where the church bell still marks the hour. Our Colnago Tuscany Bike Tour traces a six-day route from Florence to Siena through the heart of Chianti Classico, with a full day on the strade bianche and unhurried afternoons at our favorite agriturismi.

You ride hand-built Italian carbon, fitted to you on arrival, supported by two DuVine guides and a chase vehicle that is always nearby. Mornings are for climbing through the vines. Afternoons are for cellar visits, long lunches, and an espresso in a quiet piazza before the ride home.
This is the Tuscany our founders fell in love with thirty years ago, refined into a week of riding and eating that we think is among the finest we offer anywhere in the world.

items: Ride hand-built Italian carbon through the Chianti Classico region
Private cellar visits at family-run estates and historic castelli
A full day on the strade bianche, the white gravel roads of Tuscany
Walking tour of Siena with a local historian, including the Duomo
Optional truffle hunt with a local family and their trained dogs
Bistecca alla Fiorentina at Dario Cecchini's Officina, Panzano
Hand-rolled pici with wild boar ragu at a family trattoria in Florence
Tartufo bianco in season, shaved over fresh tagliolini
Pecorino di Pienza with chestnut honey and walnuts
Chianti Classico Riserva at Castello di Verrazzano
Brunello di Montalcino verticals at a small-production estate
Vermentino on the terrace at Villa Bordoni at sunset

children.text: Meet your DuVine guides at our boutique hotel in central Florence for a bike fitting and a short shakeout ride along the Arno. We gather in the late afternoon for a private walking tour of the Oltrarno, ending with a welcome dinner at a family-run trattoria where the pici is rolled by hand each morning.

itinerary.title: Into the Chianti Hills
itinerary.locations: Florence to Greve in Chianti via Impruneta

children.text: We roll out of Florence along the Greve river valley, climbing gently past olive groves and terracotta-producing villages to the medieval market square at Greve in Chianti. Lunch at a family vineyard with a tour of the cellar and a tasting of their Chianti Classico Riserva.
In the afternoon, an optional climb to the Castello di Verrazzano for those wanting extra elevation before settling into our agriturismo above the valley.

itinerary.title: Panzano and the Conca d'Oro
itinerary.locations: Greve to Panzano via Radda in Chianti

children.text: A classic Chianti loop through the Conca d'Oro, the golden basin of vines that produces some of the region's finest Sangiovese. We climb to Panzano for a butcher-shop lunch at Dario Cecchini's celebrated Officina della Bistecca, then descend through Radda along quiet ridge roads.
Return to Villa Bordoni for an evening at leisure. A swim in the pool, a glass of vermentino, the sound of cypress in the wind.

Our longest day on the bike traces the white gravel roads that gave the spring classic its name. We weave south through Castello di Brolio, ancestral seat of the Ricasoli family who codified the Chianti formula in 1872, with a cellar visit and lunch on the terrace.
A rolling afternoon brings us into the Crete Senesi, the lunar clay hills south of Siena, where we check into our hilltop estate for two nights.

A shorter ride today to make room for Siena. We cycle into the city in the morning, leaving bikes at our partner hotel for a private tour of the Piazza del Campo, the Duomo, and the contrade neighborhoods that race the Palio each summer.

Lunch is a long affair in a wine bar tucked behind the cathedral, followed by an easy spin back through the clay hills to Borgo Scopeto. Truffle hunting with a local family and their dogs is offered as an optional afternoon activity.

itinerary.title: Farewell Ride and Return to Florence
itinerary.locations: Castelnuovo Berardenga to Florence via Siena

children.text: A final morning ride through the vineyards before a celebratory lunch at a Brunello producer near Montalcino, paired with verticals of their flagship wine.
Private transfer back to Florence in the afternoon, with arrivals at the hotel in time for evening flights or an additional night in the city. Arrivederci, until next time.

itinerary.accommodation: Day use at Hotel Lungarno, Florence

children.text: Five nights of accommodation at hand-selected hotels, villas, and agriturismi. All breakfasts, five lunches, and four dinners with paired wines from the producers we visit.

Use of a premium road bike for the duration of the tour, including helmet, pedals fitted to your spec, and a saddle bag with essentials. Two DuVine guides per group of up to twelve guests, with a support vehicle on every ride.
Private cellar visits, walking tours, and entrance fees as outlined in the itinerary. Airport transfers on arrival and departure day from Florence.
In Florence, we stay at Hotel Lungarno on the south bank of the Arno, a Ferragamo family property with rooms looking onto the Ponte Vecchio. Quiet, central, and full of original Florentine art.

In Chianti, Villa Bordoni is a restored seventeenth-century stone house above Greve, with terraced gardens, a saltwater pool, and twelve individually decorated rooms. Dinner here is one of the highlights of the tour.
In the Crete Senesi, Borgo Scopeto Relais is a Sangiovese-producing estate dating to the twelfth century, with a quiet pool overlooking the vineyards and a private chapel on the grounds.

This is a moderate tour. Daily distances range from 42 to 72 kilometers with 560 to 1280 meters of climbing per day. Roads are paved tarmac with one day on the strade bianche, the well-maintained gravel sectors made famous by the spring classic.

Climbs are steady rather than steep, with most ascents in the four to seven percent range. Guests should be comfortable riding three to four hours per day with stops, and have ridden similar terrain at home before joining.
Our support vehicle is always nearby. Skipping a climb, shortening a day, or riding only the section you want is welcome and expected.

Cycling kit for five riding days: bib shorts, jerseys, a light rain jacket, and arm warmers for cool mornings. Shoes with SPD-SL or SPD cleats; let us know your preference at booking and we will fit pedals accordingly.
A change of clothes for evenings. Tuscan restaurants are relaxed but considered. Linen, light layers, comfortable walking shoes for cobblestones in Siena and Florence.
A valid passport, an EU adapter, and a curiosity for what is on your plate. Everything else, we provide.

seo.metaTitle: Colnago Tuscany Bike Tour | Six Days in Chianti | DuVine
seo.metaDescription: Ride hand-built Italian carbon through Chianti Classico and the Crete Senesi on a six-day luxury bike tour from Florence to Siena. Private cellars, agriturismo stays, expert guides.
